channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Eagles send Gardner-Johnson to Texans. Vikings bolster offensive, defensive lines in NFL free agency
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles' top-ranked defense is getting a major makeover a month after ending Patrick Mahomes' bid to lead the Kansas City Chiefs to a third consecutive Super Bowl title.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pacers' Tyrese Haliburton converts last-second 'football play' into 4 points to stun Bucks
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Tyrese Haliburton described it as “a football play” — and he converted the extra point, too.
item.dc:creator: MARK AMBROGI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No Donovan Mitchell? No problem for Cavaliers as they tie their team record with 15th straight win
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The wins and accomplishments keep piling up for the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers. They’ve been so frequent that players are having a hard time keeping track. “What did we do? Oh, we clinched the Central Division,” center Jarrett Allen said, chuckling. “That’s something.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Injured Brad Marchand returns to the Boston ice with the Panthers
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Brad Marchand stepped back onto the ice at the TD Garden on Tuesday — this time in a Panthers practice jersey as the longtime Bruin returned to Boston for the first time since he was traded to Florida.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Junior Bridgeman, businessman and basketball standout for Louisville and Milwaukee Bucks, dies at 71
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Junior Bridgeman, a basketball standout who led Louisville to a Final Four, starred for the NBA's Milwaukee Bucks and then launched an even more successful career as a businessman with stakes in restaurants, publishing and the Bucks franchise, died Tuesday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA-leading Cavaliers beat Nets 109-104 for their second 15-game win streak of season
item.description: Darius Garland scored 18 of his 30 points in the fourth quarter as the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers rallied for a 109-104 victory over the Brooklyn Nets, matching the longest winning streak in franchise history at 15.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulik, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Aztecs Men's Basketball to face Boise State in Mountain West Tournament Quarterfinals, Magoon Gwath status day to day
item.description: 4-seed Aztecs Men's Basketball to face 5-seed Boise State in Mountain West Tournament Quarterfinals, 7-foot forward Magoon Gwath status day to day.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Former NBA star Dwight Howard blames LeBron James for his Lakers exit after championship run
item.description: Former NBA star Dwight Howard did not resign with the Lakers after he helped the franchise win a title, and he claims one of his ex-teammates is responsible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PSG ousts Liverpool from Champions League on penalties to join Barcelona, Bayern and Inter in QF
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Gianluigi Donnarumma came through and Paris Saint-Germain defeated Liverpool in a penalty shootout to join Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Inter Milan in the quarterfinals of the Champions League on Tuesday. PSG beat Liverpool 1-0 on the night to level the aggregate score at 1-1.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boys Lacrosse: Carlsbad 11, Granite Hills 7
item.description: EL CAJON (KUSI)- Boys Lacrosse action on Monday night in El Cajon with Granite Hills hosting Carlsbad. The Lancers win a very good game 11-7. Despite Carson Kowalsky, just a junior having a great game, scoring four goals for Granite Hills.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin cuts funding to Maine universities as state defies order to ban trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The USDA announced Tuesday it is halting federal funding to the University of Maine System due to the state's ongoing battle for transgender athlete inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scottie Scheffler looking at a blank canvas as he goes for a 3rd straight Players Championship
item.description: PONTE VEDRA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The Players Championship has four decades of history at TPC Sawgrass suggesting it doesn't favor a particular style of golf — power player or pea shooter, great short game or consummate iron player. And then Scottie Scheffler came along.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: QB Daniel Jones agrees to a 1-year, $14 million deal with the Colts, AP source says
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Indianapolis Colts wanted someone to challenge quarterback Anthony Richardson for the starting job. They opted for Daniel Jones.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The NBA's Southeast Division is struggling. There's still hope for the Hawks, Magic and Heat
item.description: This fall will mark the 10th anniversary of the NBA changing its playoff rules and not guaranteeing a division champion a top-four seed regardless of record. This season's Southeast Division is a good reason why that change was necessary.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southwestern League basketball All-Conference teams announced
item.description: It was a wild season filled with buzzer-beaters and many other incredible moments, as we congratulate the great players honored on this year's Southwestern League All-Conference Teams.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: Justin Fields was passed over by the New York Jets four years ago in the NFL draft. Now he's expected to be their No. 1 quarterback. Fields and the Jets agreed Monday to a two-year contract worth $40 million, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles 'enthusiastically accepted' President Trump's White House invite after Super Bowl win, official says
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles have "enthusiastically accepted" President Trump's White House invite to celebrate the team's Super Bowl LIX victory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Report: Bears sign DE Dayo Odeyingbo to 3-year deal
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have signed defensive end Dayo Odeyingbo to a three-year deal, according to NFL insider Ian Rapoport. The 25-year-old was a second-round pick by the Colts in 2021 and addresses a need the team had on the defensive line.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Central, GJ, Fruita baseball start 2025 with wins as Palisade falls on day 1 of the Bill Fanning Classic
item.description: It was a busy first day of baseball action across the Grand Valley on day one of the Bill Fanning Classic.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Utah keeps rolling with 4-2 victory at Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (ABC4 Sports) - Karel Vejmelka made 38 saves for the Utah Hockey Club, who scored three straight goals to rally for a 4-2 win against the Detroit Red Wings at Little Caesars Arena on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco State University cutting men's soccer and 2 other sports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- San Francisco State University announced plans on Thursday to cut three sports programs as part of its budget planning. The announced cuts follow a budget meeting last week that was "particularly difficult," according to an open letter posted by SFSU President Lynn Mahoney.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Utes hire Alex Jensen as head basketball coach
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Alex Jensen is coming home to Utah. Jensen was hired as the University of Utah's 17th head basketball coach Thursdaay, replacing Craig Smith, who was fired last month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Men charged in deaths of 3 Chiefs fans found dead in backyard
item.description: Ricky Johnson, 38; Clayton McGeeney, 36; and David Harrington, 37, were all found dead in their friend’s Kansas City backyard on January 9, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Bulls Fest 2025: Team announces dates, registration open for 3v3 tournament
item.description: CHICAGO — Registration is now open for Bulls Fest 2025, which will be held outdoors on the United Center campus from Aug. 23-24, the team announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Chris Collins shares special basketball bond with son Ryan at Northwestern
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. - Two hours before tipoff Monday at Welsh-Ryan Arena, Ryan Collins had the ball in his hands, showing off his shot, a similar scene to another coach’s son some four decades earlier. “He shows off before the game, just like I used to do,” said Northwestern head coach Chris Collins.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9
